mnemonics.title: Preorder Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Preorder = Root First. Think 'R' for Root and 'P' for Preorder - both start together!

mnemonics.title: Postorder Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Postorder = Root Last. Remember: POST = AFTER = ROOT AFTER children.

mnemonics.title: Inorder for BST
mnemonics.content: Inorder = In SORTED order! Left → Root → Right gives ascending order in BST.
For tree deletion: Remember 'Leaves first, then parents' - children must be freed before parent (Postorder).
mnemonics.title: Tree Deletion Memory

acronyms.usage: Traverses deep into a branch before backtracking. Includes Preorder, Inorder, Postorder.
Explores level by level. Same as Level Order Traversal.

visualTips.visualization: Draw the tree, then draw vertical lines (for inorder/preorder/postorder) or horizontal lines (for level order) to show visit order.
Visualize recursion as a stack of boxes. Each recursive call adds a box, when returning, remove the top box.
Imagine a pipeline: nodes enter from back, leave from front. First in = first out, like people at a ticket counter.
Imagine a stack of plates: add (push) new plates on top, remove (pop) from top - Last In, First Out.

analogies.analogy: Arrays and linked lists are like a single-file line, while trees are like an organizational hierarchy (company org chart).
Inorder on BST is like reading pages of a book in order - left page (left subtree), current page (root), right page (right subtree).
Preorder is like photocopying: get the original first (root), then copy its attachments (children).
Postorder is like dismantling a building: demolish rooms (children) first, then the building (root).
Level order is like checking exams row by row - finish one row completely before moving to the next.
